primary_meaning: nuclear fission, splitting of atomic nuclei releasing energy
secondary_meaning: the act of splitting or dividing
usage_notes: Use in physics contexts, often with 'nucléaire', 'de l'uranium'
common_collocations: fission nucléaire, fission de l'uranium, réaction de fission
image_context: Nuclear fission reaction with energy release representing atom splitting
example_1: La fission de l'uranium libère une quantité immense d'énergie nucléaire.
example_1_translation: The fission of uranium releases an immense amount of nuclear energy.
example_2: Les réacteurs nucléaires exploitent le phénomène de fission pour produire de l'électricité.
example_2_translation: Nuclear reactors exploit the phenomenon of fission to produce electricity.
example_3: La réaction en chaîne de fission contrôlée libère de l'énergie utile.
example_3_translation: The controlled chain reaction of fission releases useful energy.
pronunciation_tips: fi-sjON. Two syllables with nasal ending: fi-SJYON
memory_hook: Fission - from Latin fissio (splitting) - the splitting of atomic nucleus

primary_meaning: nuclear fusion, combining of atomic nuclei to release energy
secondary_meaning: the act of joining or blending together
usage_notes: Use in physics and energy contexts, often with 'nucléaire'
common_collocations: fusion nucléaire, fusion thermonucléaire, énergie de fusion
image_context: Stellar fusion in the sun and energy release representing atomic nuclei combining
example_1: La fusion nucléaire est le processus fondamental qui alimente le soleil.
example_1_translation: Nuclear fusion is the fundamental process that powers the sun.
example_2: Les scientifiques cherchent à maîtriser la fusion pour une énergie propre et illimitée.
example_2_translation: Scientists seek to master fusion for clean and unlimited energy.
example_3: La réaction de fusion libère plus d'énergie que la fission nucléaire.
example_3_translation: The fusion reaction releases more energy than nuclear fission.
pronunciation_tips: fy-zjON. Two syllables with nasal ending: fy-ZYJON
memory_hook: Fusion - from Latin fusio (melting) - melting of atomic nuclei together
common_mistakes: Fusion combines nuclei, fission splits them - opposite processes

primary_meaning: radioactivity, spontaneous emission of radiation from unstable nuclei
common_collocations: radioactivité naturelle, radioactivité artificielle, déchets radioactifs
image_context: Radioactive emission and radiation representing nuclear instability
example_1: La radioactivité naturelle existe depuis la formation même de la Terre.
example_1_translation: Natural radioactivity has existed since Earth's very formation.
example_2: Les déchets radioactifs nécessitent un stockage sécurisé pendant des siècles.
example_2_translation: Radioactive waste requires secure storage for centuries.
example_3: La découverte de la radioactivité a révolutionné la physique moderne.
example_3_translation: The discovery of radioactivity revolutionized modern physics.
pronunciation_tips: ra-djo-ak-ti-vi-té. Six syllables with stress on last: ra-djo-ak-ti-vi-TÉ
memory_hook: Radioactivité - radio (radiation) + activité (activity) - active radiation emission
common_mistakes: Radioactivité is the property; radioactif is the adjective describing things

primary_meaning: asymptote, a line that a curve approaches but never touches
secondary_meaning: a straight line that a curve approaches as one variable approaches infinity
common_collocations: asymptote horizontale, asymptote verticale, comportement asymptotique
image_context: Mathematical graph showing asymptotic behavior of curves
example_1: La courbe se rapproche continuellement de l'asymptote sans jamais l'atteindre.
example_1_translation: The curve continually approaches the asymptote without ever reaching it.
example_2: En analyse, les asymptotes permettent d'étudier le comportement limite des fonctions.
example_2_translation: In analysis, asymptotes allow studying the limit behavior of functions.
example_3: Cette fonction a une asymptote verticale à x=2 et une asymptote horizontale à y=1.
example_3_translation: This function has a vertical asymptote at x=2 and a horizontal asymptote at y=1.
pronunciation_tips: a-sèn-ptot. Three syllables with stress on second: a-SÈN-ptot
memory_hook: Asymptote - from Greek a (not) + symptote (falling together) - lines that don't meet
common_mistakes: An asymptote is approached infinitely close but never actually reached

primary_meaning: topology, study of properties preserved under continuous deformation
secondary_meaning: the spatial arrangement or configuration of elements
usage_notes: Use in advanced mathematics contexts, with 'espace', 'propriétés'
common_collocations: topologie de l'espace, propriétés topologiques, espace topologique
image_context: Abstract topological shapes and continuous deformations representing topology
example_1: La topologie traite des espaces et de leurs propriétés géométriques fondamentales.
example_1_translation: Topology deals with spaces and their fundamental geometric properties.
example_2: Un donut et une tasse sont topologiquement équivalents car ils ont le même nombre de trous.
example_2_translation: A donut and a cup are topologically equivalent as they have the same number of holes.
example_3: Les propriétés topologiques sont préservées lors de déformations continues.
example_3_translation: Topological properties are preserved during continuous deformations.
pronunciation_tips: to-po-lo-ji. Four syllables with stress on third: to-po-LO-ji
memory_hook: Topologie - topos (place) + logos (study) - study of spatial properties
common_mistakes: Topology allows stretching, but not tearing or gluing - continuous deformations only

primary_meaning: differential, a mathematical operation relating to derivatives
secondary_meaning: the small change in a variable
common_collocations: équation différentielle, calcul différentiel, opérateur différentiel
synonyms: dérivée, calcul infinitésimal, opérateur de dérivation
image_context: Mathematical equations representing differential calculus concepts
example_1: Les équations différentielles modélisent l'évolution de tous les systèmes dynamiques.
example_1_translation: Differential equations model the evolution of all dynamic systems.
example_2: Le calcul différentiel fut inventé indépendamment par Newton et Leibniz.
example_2_translation: Differential calculus was independently invented by Newton and Leibniz.
example_3: La solution générale d'une équation différentielle contient des constantes arbitraires.
example_3_translation: The general solution of a differential equation contains arbitrary constants.
pronunciation_tips: dif-fé-ren-sjèl. Four syllables with stress on second: dif-FÉ-ren-sjèl
memory_hook: Différentielle - différence + ielle - all about tiny differences and derivatives

primary_meaning: morphology, study of word structure and formation rules
secondary_meaning: the structure of an organism or language system
common_collocations: morphologie linguistique, morphologie des mots, analyse morphologique
image_context: Word structure and breakdown representing morphological analysis
example_1: La morphologie s'intéresse principalement à la structure interne des mots.
example_1_translation: Morphology primarily concerns the internal structure of words.
example_2: En linguistique, la morphologie analyse les morphèmes et leurs combinaisons possibles.
example_2_translation: In linguistics, morphology analyzes morphemes and their possible combinations.
example_3: La morphologie du français montre des patterns réguliers de formation de pluriels.
example_3_translation: French morphology shows regular patterns of plural formation.
pronunciation_tips: mor-fo-lo-ji. Four syllables with stress on third: mor-fo-LO-ji
memory_hook: Morphologie - morphe (form) + logia (study) - study of word forms
common_mistakes: Morphology (structure) vs phonology (sounds) - don't confuse them

primary_meaning: semantics, study of meaning in language and communication
secondary_meaning: the branch of linguistics dealing with meaning
common_collocations: sémantique formelle, sémantique linguistique, analyse sémantique
synonyms: étude du sens, signification, théorie du sens
image_context: Meaning interpretation and semantic relationships in language
example_1: La sémantique examine comment les mots et phrases acquièrent leur signification.
example_1_translation: Semantics examines how words and phrases acquire their meaning.
example_2: En sémantique formelle, on utilise des modèles logiques pour représenter le sens.
example_2_translation: In formal semantics, logical models are used to represent meaning.
example_3: L'analyse sémantique révèle les connections entre mots apparentés.
example_3_translation: Semantic analysis reveals connections between related words.
pronunciation_tips: sé-man-tik. Three syllables with stress on first: SÉ-man-tik
memory_hook: Sémantique - semantikos (significant) - all about significant meaning
common_mistakes: Semantics (meaning) vs syntax (structure) - study different things

primary_meaning: phonology, study of sound systems and phonemes in languages
secondary_meaning: the sound system of a particular language
common_collocations: phonologie générale, phonologie du français, analyse phonologique
antonyms: phonétique (may relate to physical aspects more than system)
image_context: Sound waves and phonological systems representing language sounds
example_1: La phonologie étudie les patterns de sons distinctifs d'une langue donnée.
example_1_translation: Phonology studies the distinctive sound patterns of a given language.
example_2: Les phonologues analysent comment les sons varient selon le contexte linguistique.
example_2_translation: Phonologists analyze how sounds vary according to linguistic context.
example_3: La phonologie du français diffère significativement de celle de l'anglais.
example_3_translation: French phonology differs significantly from English phonology.
pronunciation_tips: fo-no-lo-ji. Four syllables with stress on third: fo-no-LO-ji
memory_hook: Phonologie - phone (sound) + logia (study) - study of sound systems
common_mistakes: Phonology (system) vs phonetics (physical sounds) - different scales

primary_meaning: irony, expression of meaning through contradiction or subverted expectations
secondary_meaning: a situation where outcome is opposite to expected
common_collocations: ironie de la situation, ironie dramatique, ironie verbale
image_context: Sarcastic expressions and ironic situations representing irony
example_1: L'ironie de la situation était que le pompier causa accidentellement l'incendie.
example_1_translation: The irony of the situation was that the firefighter accidentally caused the fire.
example_2: Voltaire employait l'ironie comme arme philosophique puissante contre l'intolérance.
example_2_translation: Voltaire used irony as a powerful philosophical weapon against intolerance.
example_3: L'ironie dramatique survient quand le lecteur sait quelque chose que les personnages ignorent.
example_3_translation: Dramatic irony occurs when the reader knows something the characters don't.
pronunciation_tips: i-ro-ni. Three syllables with stress on second: i-RO-ni
memory_hook: Ironie - from Greek eironeia (feigned ignorance) - saying opposite of what's meant
common_mistakes: Irony is not simply sarcasm - it's about contradiction between expectation and reality

primary_meaning: metaphor, figure of speech comparing two unlike things without 'like' or 'as'
common_collocations: métaphore filée, métaphore mélangée, métaphore morte
image_context: Abstract imagery representing metaphorical connections and expression
example_1: La vie est un voyage selon une métaphore commune en littérature romantique.
example_1_translation: Life is a journey according to a common metaphor in Romantic literature.
example_2: Les métaphores filées parcourent toute l'œuvre poétique majeure de Baudelaire.
example_2_translation: Extended metaphors run throughout Baudelaire's major poetic work.
example_3: Cette métaphore de 'la mort comme sommeil' apparaît dans de nombreux poèmes.
example_3_translation: This metaphor of 'death as sleep' appears in numerous poems.
pronunciation_tips: mé-ta-for. Three syllables with stress on first: MÉ-ta-for
memory_hook: Métaphore - meta (transfer) + pherein (to carry) - transferring meaning between things
common_mistakes: Metaphor is implicit; simile uses 'like' or 'as' - don't confuse them

primary_meaning: allegory, symbolic representation of abstract ideas through concrete narrative
secondary_meaning: a story or picture with a hidden meaning or message
usage_notes: Use with 'de', describing what abstract concept is represented
common_collocations: allégorie politique, allégorie religieuse, allégorie morale
image_context: Symbolic representation with deeper meaning representing allegory
example_1: La Boétie employait l'allégorie dans son Discours de la servitude volontaire.
example_1_translation: La Boétie used allegory in his Discourse on Voluntary Servitude.
example_2: La quête du Graal constitue une puissante allégorie de la recherche spirituelle.
example_2_translation: The Grail quest constitutes a powerful allegory of spiritual search.
example_3: Dante utilise l'allégorie pour représenter le voyage de l'âme vers le divin.
example_3_translation: Dante uses allegory to represent the soul's journey toward the divine.
pronunciation_tips: a-lé-go-ri. Four syllables with stress on second: a-LÉ-go-ri
memory_hook: Allégorie - allo (other) + agoreuein (to speak) - speaking about something else
common_mistakes: Allegory (full narrative with hidden meaning) vs metaphor (single comparison)

primary_meaning: disintegration, spontaneous decay of unstable atomic nuclei
secondary_meaning: the breaking apart or crumbling of something
common_collocations: désintégration radioactive, désintégration alpha, taux de désintégration
image_search_query: désintégration radioactive particule alpha bêta gamma
image_context: Atomic decay and particle emission representing nuclear disintegration
example_1: La désintégration radioactive émet des particules alpha, bêta ou gamma distinctes.
example_1_translation: Radioactive disintegration emits distinct alpha, beta, or gamma particles.
example_2: Le taux de désintégration permet de calculer précisément la demi-vie d'un isotope.
example_2_translation: The disintegration rate allows precisely calculating the half-life of an isotope.
example_3: La désintégration du carbone-14 est utilisée en archéologie pour dater les fossiles.
example_3_translation: The disintegration of carbon-14 is used in archaeology to date fossils.
pronunciation_tips: dé-zin-té-gra-sjON. Five syllables with nasal ending on last
memory_hook: Désintégration - dés (un) + intégration (making whole) - breaking apart
common_mistakes: Disintegration is spontaneous; fragmentation is breaking apart deliberately

primary_meaning: isotope, a variant of an element with different neutron number
secondary_meaning: an atom having same atomic number but different mass number
usage_notes: Use with element names, stability descriptors, applications
common_collocations: isotope stable, isotope radioactif, isotope de l'uranium
image_context: Atomic structure showing isotope variations in neutron count
example_1: L'isotope de l'uranium-235 est utilisé directement comme combustible nucléaire.
example_1_translation: The isotope uranium-235 is used directly as nuclear fuel.
example_2: Les isotopes stables sont largement utilisés en datation radiométrique précise.
example_2_translation: Stable isotopes are widely used in precise radiometric dating.
example_3: Différents isotopes d'un même élément ont des propriétés chimiques similaires mais des masses différentes.
example_3_translation: Different isotopes of the same element have similar chemical properties but different masses.
pronunciation_tips: i-zo-top. Three syllables with stress on second: i-ZO-top
memory_hook: Isotope - iso (same) + topos (place) - same place in periodic table, different mass
common_mistakes: Isotopes have same atomic number but different mass numbers

primary_meaning: hermeneutics, theory and practice of textual interpretation
secondary_meaning: the method of interpreting meaning in texts and communication
common_collocations: herméneutique biblique, herméneutique littéraire, herméneutique philosophique
image_context: Text analysis and interpretation representing hermeneutic methodology
example_1: L'herméneutique biblique a considérablement évolué depuis les Pères de l'Église ancienne.
example_1_translation: Biblical hermeneutics has considerably evolved since the ancient Church Fathers.
example_2: Gadamer a révolutionné l'herméneutique philosophique au cours du vingtième siècle.
example_2_translation: Gadamer revolutionized philosophical hermeneutics in the twentieth century.
example_3: L'herméneutique contemporaine examine comment les lecteurs construisent le sens.
example_3_translation: Contemporary hermeneutics examines how readers construct meaning.
pronunciation_tips: ér-mé-nœ-tik. Four syllables with stress on third: ér-mé-NØ-tik
memory_hook: Herméneutique - Hermes (messenger god) - interpreting messages and meaning
common_mistakes: Hermeneutics is a methodology, not just casual reading interpretation
